description: Taxonomy of asset types found in OT environments, with security characteristics per type. Used to drive asset inventory, zone assignment, and control applicability.

securityProfile.nativeAuth: Limited — newer models (post-2015) have password protection; legacy have none
securityProfile.patchable: Firmware updates via vendor only; require testing; often years behind
securityProfile.encryptionSupport: OPC-UA with signing available on modern PLCs; legacy have none
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: High — directly controls physical process; Stuxnet-class attacks target PLCs
compensatingControls: Change management for all program modifications
Jump server for remote engineering access

securityProfile.nativeAuth: DNP3 Secure Authentication v5 available but rarely deployed; most use unauthenticated DNP3
securityProfile.patchable: Difficult — remote sites; requires vendor involvement; often years between updates
securityProfile.encryptionSupport: DNP3 SAv5 supports HMAC-based authentication; TLS for TCP transport in newer deployments
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: High — typically in remote locations with poor physical security and direct SCADA connectivity
compensatingControls: Physical tamper detection at remote site

securityProfile.nativeAuth: DCS vendor proprietary — varies; engineering access typically password-protected at workstation level
securityProfile.patchable: Vendor-managed; major version upgrades required for significant security patches; long lifecycle
securityProfile.encryptionSupport: Newer DCS support OPC-UA with security; older systems limited
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: High — controls continuous process; single DCS may control entire facility

securityProfile.nativeAuth: TriStation protocol supports key switch (physical hardware key) and software password; limited network authentication
securityProfile.patchable: Extremely difficult — SIS patch requires process shutdown; vendors limit available patches to maintain SIL certification
securityProfile.encryptionSupport: Very limited — safety protocols prioritise determinism over encryption
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: Extreme — TRITON/TRISIS attack (2017) demonstrated targeted SIS exploitation causing potential mass casualty risk
compensatingControls: Physical air gap from DCS and IT networks
Hardware key switch for engineering access mode
Data diode for outbound status data only
No remote access under any circumstances
Two-person rule for all SIS modifications

securityProfile.nativeAuth: Application-level authentication; typically Windows-integrated or proprietary user management
securityProfile.patchable: Windows OS patches must be vendor-tested; HMI application patches less frequent
securityProfile.encryptionSupport: OPC-UA supports TLS; web-based HMIs support HTTPS; older native clients limited
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: High — HMI software has extensive CVE history; internet-exposed HMIs are a major attack vector
compensatingControls: Remove unused features (web server if not needed)
No internet access from HMI workstation

securityProfile.nativeAuth: Windows authentication plus engineering software licensing; some tools require dongle
securityProfile.patchable: Windows patches must be vendor-qualified; engineering software updates vendor-managed
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: Extreme — provides direct PLC programming access; Stuxnet spread via infected engineering project files
compensatingControls: PAM tool for all engineering sessions
No email or internet browsing on EWS
Jump server as only remote access path

securityProfile.nativeAuth: Windows authentication or vendor proprietary; API key authentication for programmatic access
securityProfile.patchable: Regular software updates available; Windows patches must be managed
securityProfile.encryptionSupport: HTTPS for web interfaces; TLS for data connections; PI Cloud for cloud replication
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: Medium-High — historian is a data aggregation point bridging OT and IT; compromise provides insight into process operations and may allow data manipulation
compensatingControls: Historian in IDMZ or Level 3 with strict firewall rules
Audit logging for all historian modifications

securityProfile.nativeAuth: Management interface authentication (SSH, HTTPS); port authentication (802.1X)
securityProfile.patchable: Firmware updates available; critical for network device security
securityProfile.encryptionSupport: Management plane encryption (SSH/HTTPS); data plane typically unencrypted
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: Medium — network device vulnerabilities allow traffic interception or manipulation; unmanaged switches have no security capability
compensatingControls: Replace unmanaged switches with managed equivalents

assetTypes.name: OT/Industrial Firewall
protocols: OT protocol-aware DPI (Modbus, DNP3, EtherNet/IP, PROFINET, IEC 104)

securityProfile.patchable: Regular firmware updates critical — apply promptly
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: Low-Medium — firewall hardening essential; misconfigured firewalls are worse than no firewall (false sense of security)
compensatingControls: Use OT-aware firewalls with protocol DPI
Review and validate all permit rules
Separate management interface on dedicated management VLAN

assetTypes.name: Data Diode / Unidirectional Gateway
protocols: Protocol-specific — OPC, Modbus, historian replication, file transfer

securityProfile.nativeAuth: Hardware-enforced — physical one-way transmission; no software bypass possible
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: Very Low — hardware physics prevent reverse data flow; software components on receiving side require hardening
compensatingControls: Regular testing that reverse flow is physically impossible

securityProfile.nativeAuth: MFA supported; certificate-based authentication; session management
securityProfile.patchable: Regular updates required — remote access gateway is internet-facing
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: High — internet-facing component; primary target for initial access

assetTypes.name: OT Security Monitoring Sensor
protocols: Passive - captures: Modbus, DNP3, EtherNet/IP, PROFINET, IEC 60870, IEC 61850, BACnet, OPC-UA

securityProfile.patchable: Regular updates for protocol parsers and detection rules
securityProfile.vulnerabilityRisk: Low — passive devices do not send traffic to OT network; compromise affects visibility not control
compensatingControls: Ensure sensor cannot be used to inject traffic into OT network
Monitor sensor availability — loss of sensor coverage is a security event
